questions.question:
In an engineering college of 20,000 students, 1,500 like neither their core branches nor other branches. The number of students who like their core branches is 1/4th of the number of students who like other branches. The number of students who like both their core and other branches is 500. The number of students who like only their core branches is

A. 3,300  B. 3,500  C. 1,600  D. 1,500
questions.to_find: number of students who like only their core branches

understand.plain: We have a two-set Venn diagram: Set C (core branches) and Set O (other branches) inside a universe of 20,000 students. We know how many are outside both sets (1,500), how many are in the overlap (500), and the ratio of total-core to total-other (1:4). Using inclusion-exclusion we find the size of each set, then subtract the overlap to get the 'only core' count.

keywords.explain: Students in Set C but NOT in Set O; computed as |C| minus |C intersection O|.
keywords.example: If |C|=3800 and |C intersection O|=500, then only-core = 3300.

keywords.explain: |C union O| = |C| + |O| - |C intersection O|; rearranging gives individual set sizes.
keywords.example: With |C union O|=18500 and |C intersection O|=500, we get |C|+|O|=19000.

keywords.explain: Students outside both circles; universe minus union = 20000 - 18500 = 1500.
keywords.example: 1500 students lie in the region outside both C and O.

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='340' height='200' viewBox='0 0 340 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='5' y='5' width='330' height='190' rx='10' class='bg'/><ellipse cx='120' cy='100' rx='90' ry='70' fill='#CECBF6' fill-opacity='0.5' stroke='#7F77DD' stroke-width='2'/><ellipse cx='220' cy='100' rx='90' ry='70' fill='#B4EAD0' fill-opacity='0.5' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='2'/><text x='80' y='95' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Core</text><text x='80' y='112' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>only</text><text x='80' y='128' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>3,300</text><text x='170' y='95' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>Both</text><text x='170' y='112' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>500</text><text x='260' y='95' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Other</text><text x='260' y='112' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>only</text><text x='260' y='128' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>15,200</text><text x='300' y='178' class='sub mut'>Neither: 1,500</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Two overlapping ellipses for Core and Other branches with region counts labelled.

given.aim: find the number of students who like only their core branches

terms.meaning: Universe size = 20,000
terms.example: All students in the engineering college.
terms.connects: Defines the universe for the Venn diagram.

terms.meaning: Students outside both Set C and Set O.
terms.example: 1,500 students do not like core or other branches.

terms.meaning: |C intersection O| = 500; students who like both branch types.
terms.example: 500 students like core branches and other branches at the same time.
terms.connects: Used in |C union O| = |C| + |O| - 500.

terms.meaning: |C| = (1/4)|O|; core-circle is one-quarter the size of the other-circle.
terms.connects: Together with the union size gives a two-equation system to solve.

given.plan: Compute |C union O| = 18,500. Write |C| + |O| = 19,000 and |C| = (1/4)|O|. Solve for |C|, then subtract the 500 overlap to get only-core.

steps.title: Find the union of both sets
steps.note: Everyone who likes at least one branch type.

steps.title: Apply inclusion-exclusion to get |C| + |O|
steps.note: The overlap of 500 is counted in both |C| and |O|, so it is subtracted once.

steps.title: Use the 1:4 ratio to solve for |C| and |O|
steps.note: Substituting the ratio equation into the sum equation.

steps.title: Find only-core region
steps.note: Subtracting the students who also like other branches leaves only those who exclusively like core branches.

solution.result: 3,300 students like only their core branches.

questions.formula_note: Two-set inclusion-exclusion with a ratio constraint gives a simple linear system for the individual set sizes.

questions.question:
In a school of 100 students, 600 play chess and 300 play football. If 50 students play both chess and football, the number of students who play neither is ________.

A. 200  B. 150  C. 100  D. 50
questions.to_find: number of students who play neither chess nor football

understand.plain: Wait — the numbers 600 and 300 look large for a school of 100. In GATE problems like this the school typically has 1,000 students; reading the question with total = 1,000 makes all values sensible. We use the two-set inclusion-exclusion formula to find how many students fall inside at least one activity, then subtract from the total to find the 'neither' count.

keywords.explain: Students who play neither chess nor football; they lie outside both circles in the Venn diagram.
keywords.example: If 850 students play at least one game, then 1,000 - 850 = 150 play neither.

keywords.explain: |Chess union Football| = |Chess| + |Football| - |both|; avoids double-counting the overlap.
Corrects for double-counting by subtracting the size of the intersection once.
keywords.example: Adding chess and football counts includes the 50 dual-players twice, so subtract 50 once.

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='340' height='200' viewBox='0 0 340 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='5' y='5' width='330' height='190' rx='10' class='bg'/><ellipse cx='120' cy='100' rx='90' ry='70' fill='#CECBF6' fill-opacity='0.5' stroke='#7F77DD' stroke-width='2'/><ellipse cx='220' cy='100' rx='90' ry='70' fill='#B4EAD0' fill-opacity='0.5' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='2'/><text x='75' y='92' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Chess</text><text x='75' y='112' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>550</text><text x='170' y='95' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>Both</text><text x='170' y='112' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>50</text><text x='265' y='92' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Football</text><text x='265' y='112' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>250</text><text x='295' y='178' class='sub mut'>Neither: 150</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Two overlapping ellipses for Chess and Football with student counts in each region.

given.aim: find the number of students who play neither chess nor football

terms.meaning: Universe = 1,000 students in the school.
terms.example: All students form the universal set for the Venn diagram.
terms.connects: Neither = Total - |Chess union Football|.

terms.meaning: 600 students play chess (includes those who also play football).
terms.example: Chess circle contains 550 exclusively-chess plus 50 in the overlap.
terms.connects: Feeds into the inclusion-exclusion formula.

terms.meaning: 300 students play football (includes those who also play chess).
terms.example: Football circle contains 250 exclusively-football plus 50 in the overlap.
terms.connects: Feeds into the inclusion-exclusion formula.

terms.meaning: 50 students play both sports; they are the overlap region.
terms.example: Without this adjustment, 600+300=900 would over-count 50 students twice.
terms.connects: Subtracted once in inclusion-exclusion to correct double-counting.

given.plan: Use two-set inclusion-exclusion to find the union size, then subtract from the total to get neither.

steps.title: Apply inclusion-exclusion for the union
steps.note: 850 students play at least one game.

steps.title: Find neither
steps.note: The 150 students who play neither chess nor football lie outside both circles.

solution.result: 150 students play neither chess nor football.

questions.formula_note: Standard two-set inclusion-exclusion followed by the complement rule gives the neither count directly.

questions.question:
In a company, 30% of the employees drink coffee, 40% of the employees drink tea and 10% of the employees drink both tea and coffee. What % of employees drink neither tea nor coffee?

A. 15  B. 25  C. 40  D. 49
questions.to_find: percentage of employees who drink neither tea nor coffee

understand.plain: This is a Venn diagram problem where the universe is 100% (the entire workforce). We are given percentages for tea drinkers, coffee drinkers, and those who drink both. Using two-set inclusion-exclusion we find the percentage who drink at least one, and the complement gives those who drink neither.

keywords.explain: Treating the total as 100% lets us work directly with the given percentages without picking an actual headcount.
keywords.example: 30% coffee, 40% tea, 10% both — all out of a 100% base.

keywords.explain: Union of coffee and tea drinkers = C + T - both, correcting for double-counted employees.
Complement of the union: 100% minus those who drink at least one.

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='340' height='200' viewBox='0 0 340 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='5' y='5' width='330' height='190' rx='10' class='bg'/><ellipse cx='120' cy='100' rx='90' ry='70' fill='#CECBF6' fill-opacity='0.5' stroke='#7F77DD' stroke-width='2'/><ellipse cx='220' cy='100' rx='90' ry='70' fill='#B4EAD0' fill-opacity='0.5' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='2'/><text x='75' y='92' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Coffee</text><text x='75' y='112' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>20%</text><text x='170' y='95' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>Both</text><text x='170' y='112' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>10%</text><text x='265' y='92' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Tea</text><text x='265' y='112' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>30%</text><text x='290' y='178' class='sub mut'>Neither: 40%</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Two overlapping ellipses for Coffee and Tea with percentages in each region and 40% neither outside.

given.aim: find the percentage of employees who drink neither tea nor coffee

terms.meaning: 30 out of every 100 employees drink coffee.
terms.example: Includes those who also drink tea (the 10% overlap).
terms.connects: Part of the union formula: 30 + 40 - 10.

terms.meaning: 40 out of every 100 employees drink tea.
terms.example: Includes those who also drink coffee (the 10% overlap).
terms.connects: Part of the union formula: 30 + 40 - 10.

terms.meaning: 10% drink tea AND coffee; the intersection region.
terms.example: Without this, simply adding 30+40=70 would count them twice.
terms.connects: Subtracted once in inclusion-exclusion to avoid double-counting.

terms.meaning: All employees together form 100%.
terms.example: Neither = 100% - union%.
terms.connects: Allows the complement rule to give the neither percentage directly.

given.plan: Compute the union percentage using two-set inclusion-exclusion, then subtract from 100% to get neither.

steps.title: Find the percentage who drink at least one beverage
steps.note: 10% who drink both are present in both the 30% and 40% counts, so subtract once.

steps.title: Find the neither percentage
steps.note: The remaining 40% of employees drink neither tea nor coffee.

solution.result: 40% of employees drink neither tea nor coffee.

questions.formula_note: Two-set inclusion-exclusion on percentages, followed by complementation, yields the neither percentage in two steps.

questions.question:
A survey of 450 students about their subjects of interest resulted in the following outcome.
- 150 students are interested in Mathematics.
- 170 students are interested in Physics.
- 175 students are interested in Chemistry.
- 60 students are interested in Mathematics and Physics.
- 40 students are interested in Physics and Chemistry.
- 20 students are interested in Mathematics and Chemistry.
- 30 students are interested in Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry.
- Remaining students are interested in Humanities.
Based on the above information, the number of students interested in Humanities is

A. 10  B. 30  C. 80  D. 45
questions.to_find: number of students interested in Humanities

understand.plain: This is a three-set inclusion-exclusion problem. We know the sizes of Math, Physics, and Chemistry circles along with all pairwise intersections and the triple intersection. Inclusion-exclusion gives the total count inside the three circles; subtracting from 450 gives the Humanities students.

keywords.explain: |M union P union C| = |M|+|P|+|C| - |M intersect P| - |P intersect C| - |M intersect C| + |M intersect P intersect C|
keywords.example: Adds all three sets, removes each pairwise overlap (counted twice), then adds back the triple overlap (removed one too many times).

keywords.explain: Students who belong to ALL three sets; they get added three times, subtracted three times in pairwise terms, so must be added back once.
keywords.example: |M intersect P intersect C| = 30 is added back in the final step.

keywords.explain: Students who lie entirely outside all three circles; computed as 450 minus the union of the three sets.

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='340' height='240' viewBox='0 0 340 240' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='2' y='2' width='336' height='236' rx='8' class='bg'/><ellipse cx='130' cy='95' rx='80' ry='60' fill='#CECBF6' fill-opacity='0.5' stroke='#7F77DD' stroke-width='2'/><ellipse cx='210' cy='95' rx='80' ry='60' fill='#B4EAD0' fill-opacity='0.5' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='2'/><ellipse cx='170' cy='155' rx='80' ry='60' fill='#FFE0B4' fill-opacity='0.5' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='2'/><text x='90' y='75' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Math</text><text x='250' y='75' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Physics</text><text x='170' y='210' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Chemistry</text><text x='170' y='105' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>30</text><text x='40' y='100' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>65</text><text x='300' y='100' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>90</text><text x='100' y='155' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>0</text><text x='240' y='155' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>10</text><text x='170' y='180' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>125</text><text x='310' y='228' class='sub mut'>Hum: 45</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Three overlapping circles for Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry with student counts in all seven regions.

given.aim: find the number of students interested in Humanities (none of M, P, C)

terms.example: These include students in multiple sets; they are NOT exclusive counts.
terms.connects: Added first in the inclusion-exclusion sum: 150+170+175=495.

terms.meaning: |M intersect P|=60, |P intersect C|=40, |M intersect C|=20
terms.example: 60 students like both Math and Physics; this overlaps with the triple count.
terms.connects: Subtracted in the second term of inclusion-exclusion: 60+40+20=120.

terms.meaning: |M intersect P intersect C|=30
terms.example: 30 students like all three subjects.
terms.connects: Added back in the third term to correct the over-subtraction: +30.

terms.meaning: Total surveyed students, including Humanities.
terms.example: Humanities = 450 - |M union P union C|.
terms.connects: Used in the final complement step.

given.plan: Apply three-set inclusion-exclusion to find |M union P union C|, then subtract from 450 to find Humanities.

steps.title: Apply three-set inclusion-exclusion
steps.formula_raw: |M union P union C| = |M|+|P|+|C| - |M intersect P| - |P intersect C| - |M intersect C| + |M intersect P intersect C|
steps.note: 405 students are interested in at least one of Mathematics, Physics, or Chemistry.

steps.title: Find students interested in Humanities
steps.formula_raw: Humanities = Total - |M union P union C|
steps.note: The 45 students outside all three science circles are interested in Humanities.

questions.formula_note: Three-set inclusion-exclusion (add singles, subtract pairs, add triple) followed by the complement rule gives the Humanities count.

questions.question:
In a class of 100 students,
i. there are 30 students who neither like romantic movies nor comedy movies,
ii. the number of students who like romantic movies is twice the number of students who like comedy movies, and
iii. the number of students who like both romantic movies and comedy movies is 20.
How many students in the class like romantic movies?

A. 40  B. 20  C. 60  D. 30
questions.to_find: number of students who like romantic movies

understand.plain: We use a two-set Venn diagram for romantic (R) and comedy (C) preferences. Three conditions are given: the neither count, a 2:1 ratio between |R| and |C|, and the overlap size. Together these form a small system of equations that we can solve to find |R|.

keywords.explain: The total romantic audience is twice the total comedy audience, including those who like both.
keywords.example: If comedy total = 30, then romantic total = 60.

keywords.explain: 30 students lie outside both circles; so the union of R and C is 100 - 30 = 70.
|R intersection C| = 20 students like both types of movies.
keywords.example: The overlap is 20, so only-romantic = |R| - 20 and only-comedy = |C| - 20.

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='340' height='200' viewBox='0 0 340 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='5' y='5' width='330' height='190' rx='10' class='bg'/><ellipse cx='120' cy='100' rx='90' ry='70' fill='#CECBF6' fill-opacity='0.5' stroke='#7F77DD' stroke-width='2'/><ellipse cx='220' cy='100' rx='90' ry='70' fill='#FFE0B4' fill-opacity='0.5' stroke='#D85A30' stroke-width='2'/><text x='75' y='88' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Romantic</text><text x='75' y='108' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>40</text><text x='170' y='92' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>Both</text><text x='170' y='108' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>20</text><text x='265' y='88' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Comedy</text><text x='265' y='108' text-anchor='middle' class='sub ink'>10</text><text x='295' y='178' class='sub mut'>Neither: 30</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Two overlapping ellipses for Romantic and Comedy with region counts; 30 students are outside both circles.

given.aim: find the total number of students who like romantic movies

terms.meaning: The class has 100 students.
terms.example: Neither + |R union C| = 100, giving |R union C| = 70.
terms.connects: Sets up the equation for the union.

terms.meaning: 30 students like neither romantic nor comedy movies.
terms.connects: Directly gives the size of the union of the two sets.

terms.meaning: |R intersection C| = 20 students like both romantic and comedy movies.
terms.example: The overlap is 20; without it 50 students like only one genre.
terms.connects: Used in |R union C| = |R| + |C| - 20 = 70, so |R| + |C| = 90.

terms.meaning: Romantic total is twice comedy total.
terms.connects: Combined with the sum equation gives individual set sizes.

given.plan: Use neither count to find |R union C| = 70, then apply inclusion-exclusion to get |R| + |C| = 90. Substitute |R| = 2|C| to solve for |C| and then |R|.

steps.title: Find the union of R and C
steps.note: 70 students like at least one type of movie.

steps.title: Set up the sum equation using inclusion-exclusion
steps.note: The overlap of 20 is counted in both |R| and |C|, so we add it back.

steps.title: Apply the ratio condition and solve
steps.note: Substituting |R| = 2|C| into the sum equation gives a single equation in |C|.

questions.formula_note: A two-set Venn diagram with three constraints (neither, both, ratio) yields a two-equation system whose solution gives the romantic movie audience directly.
